title: Cyclophyllum coprosmoides
taxonomy: Cyclophyllum coprosmoides is classified as a species of plant in the family Rubiaceae. It belongs to the genus Cyclophyllum, which is a group of flowering plants. Within the family Rubiaceae, Cyclophyllum coprosmoides is part of the subfamily Rubioideae. The exact classification of this plant may vary depending on the source, but it is generally accepted to be a member of the Rubiaceae family. This classification is based on the plant's morphological characteristics and genetic relationships with other plants in the family.
uses: Cyclophyllum coprosmoides, a plant native to New Caledonia, has been found to possess various medicinal properties. It is used in traditional medicine to treat a range of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. The plant's leaves and bark are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it a potential treatment for wounds and infections. Research on the plant's medicinal uses is ongoing, and further studies are needed to fully understand its potential health benefits. However, some traditional healers continue to use Cyclophyllum coprosmoides in their treatments, citing its effectiveness in alleviating symptoms of various ailments.

intro: Cyclophyllum coprosmoides, also known as the 'tātāi', is a plant species native to the Pacific Islands, classified as a small tree or shrub belonging to the family Rubiaceae. The leaves and roots of the tātāi plant have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, as well as potential antiviral and antidiabetic effects. Bioactive constituents of Cyclophyllum coprosmoides include iridoids and flavonoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Traditionally, the tātāi plant is used to make various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and extracts, often used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
